beats.activity: Wake up, shower, grab coffee and breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Standard morning routine; waking at 7:30 gives time to move through the day. Yesterday's energy was 45/100 (tired but restless from pottery), so a calm start at home is appropriate.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila bike shop, avoiding SR-520 closure by taking I-5 south
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; leaving by 8am avoids the backup entirely. I-5 is the reliable route and gets her to work before the closure window.

beats.activity: Bike mechanic shift: wheel truing, cable adjustments, and customer consultations
beats.reasoning: Rainy Tuesday keeps outdoor riders away; shop work is steadier than weekend guide gigs. Meticulous focus on technical work suits her personality. Shift runs through the rain window (7am–2pm).

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a quick sandwich and coffee at a local cafe near the shop
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady at midday; staying local near the shop is practical. Quick meal fits her go-with-the-flow style and leaves time before afternoon tasks.

beats.activity: Bike mechanic shift continues: rear hub rebuild, spoke replacement, and inventory check
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon shift; rain is clearing by 2pm, but shop work remains priority on a Tuesday. Meticulous spreadsheet-keeping personality means she may tally expenses or stock during downtime.

beats.activity: Drive home to Ballard, SR-520 now reopened, taking I-5 north then surface streets
beats.reasoning: Evening commute; SR-520 closure is over by 6:30pm. I-5 north is reliable. 50min budget accounts for light rush-hour traffic and surface-street navigation into Ballard.

beats.activity: Prep and cook a simple dinner at home (pasta, veggies, olive oil)
beats.reasoning: Tired from a full day of technical work; cooking at home is grounding and fits her meticulous nature. No fancy plans on a rainy Tuesday.

beats.activity: Pottery session: throw clay at the wheel, exploring form and texture
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's pottery session was meditative and grounding; rainy evening is perfect for quiet creative time. Aligns with her personality and low energy (45/100 yesterday carries into today).

beats.activity: Wind down: shower, tidy up, review expense spreadsheet, prepare for bed
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down beat before sleep; meticulous nature means she tracks daily expenses. Shower and tidying are practical end-of-day routines. Already at home for seamless transition to sleep.

diary: Woke up to that grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you want to stay in bed with coffee. Got out anyway and hit the road early to dodge the SR-520 mess. The bridge closure had everyone jumpy this morning, but I-5 was clear enough. Spent most of the day at the shop doing the usual: truing wheels, rebuilding hubs, talking customers through their gear. There's something satisfying about getting your hands deep into a rear hub, feeling each spoke click into place. Grabbed a sandwich at the cafe near Tukwila and just sat there for a bit, watching the rain. Drive home felt lighter once the bridge reopened. Made pasta, nothing fancy, then spent the evening at the wheel throwing clay. My hands needed something softer after all those precision adjustments. The clay was stubborn tonight but that's okay—some days it just wants to be difficult. Showered off the slip and called it.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure set a cautious tone early, so I planned the commute defensively—no point fighting traffic. Work was steady and grounding; I find real focus in mechanical tasks, especially when customers trust me to fix their bikes right. The lunch break felt like a reset I needed. By evening, I was mentally fried from concentrating hard all day, so pottery was the natural move—it's my counterweight to the precision work. I chose clay over socializing because I wanted to decompress alone, to let my hands do something intuitive rather than exact. The day felt balanced between structure and release, and I ended it feeling tired but settled.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
